title: Magnetic post clampHas anyone designed a magnetic quick fix / temporary magnetic mount.

messages.author: Richard Clark
messages.text: Looking to test locations by mounting the repeater on a metal post but was thinking about a magnetic concave gripper with magnets mounted on the inside on a moving vertical angled mount to allow for different sized poles / posts.
messages.content_html: Looking to test locations by mounting the repeater on a metal post but was thinking about a magnetic concave gripper with magnets mounted on the inside on a moving vertical angled mount to allow for different sized poles / posts.

messages.author: woketothetruth
messages.text: Great idea! I could go for some of that as well!
messages.content_html: Great idea! I could go for some of that as well!

messages.author: Matt - M2MGubbins
messages.text: Hi @Richard Clark the RAKwireless unify enclosures have a range of mounting options including mag mount, pole mount etc. We sell them in the UK, please see the link below: https://www.m2mgubbins.com/category/enclosures
messages.content_html: Hi @Richard Clark the RAKwireless unify enclosures have a range of mounting options including mag mount, pole mount etc. We sell them in the UK, please see the link below: https://www.m2mgubbins.com/category/enclosures
messages.reply_html:
Richard Clark
Looking to test locations by mounting the repeater on a metal post but was thinking about a magnetic concave gripper with magnets mounted on the inside on a moving vertical angled mount to allow for different sized poles / posts.

messages.author: Richard Clark
messages.text: Thanks, these look like flat mounding for the magnets, I was thinking about a curved or right angled holder with magnets on a horizontal / angled slide to grip round the post
messages.content_html: Thanks, these look like flat mounding for the magnets, I was thinking about a curved or right angled holder with magnets on a horizontal / angled slide to grip round the post
messages.reply_html:
Matt - M2MGubbins
Hi @Richard Clark the RAKwireless unify enclosures have a range of mounting options including mag mount, pole mount etc. We sell them in the UK, please see the link below: https://www.m2mgubbins.com/category/enclosures

messages.author: dotdavid
messages.text: I used some plumbers strap and some coated magnets bolted onto it to hold a repeater steady on a pole for ages. Survived a couple of storms without budging. The strap is flexible enough to curve around the pole, but rigid enough to not sag with the weight of the unit, and obviously you can cut it to whatever size you want.
messages.content_html: I used some plumbers strap and some coated magnets bolted onto it to hold a repeater steady on a pole for ages. Survived a couple of storms without budging. The strap is flexible enough to curve around the pole, but rigid enough to not sag with the weight of the unit, and obviously you can cut it to whatever size you want.

messages.author: Richard Clark
messages.text: That’s a great solution to the problem.
messages.content_html: That’s a great solution to the problem.
